one_line: A Meluhhan merchant–sailor from Lothal’s Harappan ports, voyaging via Dilmun and Magan toward Mesopotamian cities.

appearance.age_look: mid-20s, lean and wiry, used to hard physical work at sea
appearance.face: oval face with strong cheekbones, medium-brown Indus-valley complexion, straight nose, slightly arched brows, a few faint sun-freckles, default expression focused and alert with a hint of quiet confidence
appearance.hair: thick black hair oiled and drawn back into a short, neat topknot at the crown held by a plain dark wooden pin, with the sides and back kept close-cropped; no beard, only a subtle shadow of stubble on the chin
appearance.costume: Simple, era-appropriate Harappan sailor–merchant outfit: a knee-length wrapped cotton kilt in undyed off-white tied at the waist with a narrow dark indigo-dyed cloth sash knotted on his left hip, the kilt’s front panel folded diagonally; an upper body covered by a light, sleeveless cotton mantle in pale tan draped from the left shoulder across the chest and under the right arm so the torso is modestly covered while leaving the right shoulder more mobile; on his feet, flat leather sandals in dark brown with a toe loop and double straps around the ankle; jewellery kept practical—a single carved red carnelian bead on a thin dark cord around his neck, and a narrow hammered copper bangle on his right wrist; for headgear in harsh sun he often wears a simple pale-ochre cloth headband tied at the back under the topknot, the loose ends trailing slightly, but the band never covers the knot itself; a small, plain woven reed pouch in muted brown hangs from his sash on the right side for seals and weights; textiles are plain-weave with very subtle, thin horizontal striping in slightly darker thread on the kilt, barely visible but giving texture.
appearance.signature: A rectangular baked-clay Harappan-style seal stone, about the size of his palm, drilled so it hangs vertically from his neck cord beside the carnelian bead, its carved boss gripped between his fingers when he’s sealing cargo, accompanied by a small bundle of conical clay sealings in his sash pouch.

ref_desc: Harappan Sea Trader of Lothal is a lean, mid-20s Meluhhan sailor with medium-brown skin, black topknot, off-white kilt, pale tan mantle, indigo sash, brown sandals, carnelian bead and hanging seal on a cord, and copper bangle in a muted natural palette.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of the Harappan Sea Trader of Lothal: a lean, mid-20s Meluhhan merchant–sailor with an oval face, medium-brown Indus-valley complexion, strong cheekbones, straight nose, and focused, quietly confident expression; his thick black hair is oiled and drawn into a short topknot at the crown secured by a dark wooden pin, with sides and back close-cropped and a faint stubble shadow on the chin; he wears a knee-length wrapped off-white cotton kilt with a diagonal front fold, tied by a narrow indigo cloth sash knotted on his left hip, subtle thin horizontal striping giving texture, and a light sleeveless pale-tan cotton mantle draped from his left shoulder across the chest and under the right arm so his torso is fully and modestly covered while leaving the right shoulder more mobile; his feet have dark brown flat leather sandals with a toe loop and ankle straps; accessories are a single carved red-carnelian bead and a rectangular baked-clay Harappan-style seal hanging together from a dark cord on his chest, a narrow hammered copper bangle on his right wrist, and a simple pale-ochre cloth headband tied behind the topknot with short trailing ends; a small plain woven reed pouch in muted brown hangs from his sash on the right side, slightly bulging with clay sealings; show his signature seal clearly in one hand as if he is about to stamp cargo, and keep the overall colour palette to off-white, pale tan, indigo blue, dark brown, and coppery red, with clean, historically grounded, non-fantastical garments and accessories.
